search_queries_used: site:doh.gov.ae obstructive sleep apnea guideline UAE
site:dha.gov.ae sleep apnea clinical guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng202 obstructive sleep apnoea assessment diagnostic follow-up
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Commercial pages were not accepted as authoritative sources.
Device settings and autonomous treatment instructions were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for people over 16 years; the legacy workflow has no age restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for adult initial assessment, sleep-service diagnosis, recommendation-qualified treatment discussion, and monitoring.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local sleep-service, diagnostic, and driving governance.
recency_verification: The current official NG202 OSAHS recommendations were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG202 remains the current official NICE guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult symptom-profile, obesity context, Epworth, BMI, neck-circumference assessment context, presets, and structured history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped severe sleepiness, driving-risk, oxygen information, referral, and driving-discussion documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports referral information on assessment scores, functional sleepiness, comorbidities, occupational or driving risk, and oxygen saturation or blood gases if available.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed sleep-study and oximetry fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed respiratory polygraphy, home oximetry where appropriate, polysomnography in the specified context, and use of sleep-study results by clinicians to diagnose and grade OSAHS.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-discussed weight, sleep-hygiene, CPAP, and lifestyle fields only in recommendation-qualified OSAHS contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports tailored clinician discussion of weight, smoking, alcohol, sleep hygiene, and CPAP in recommendation-qualified diagnosed OSAHS contexts; no medicine, device setting, or autonomous treatment output is mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped arranged follow-up and ongoing symptoms, Epworth, vigilance, adherence, sleep-hygiene, and sleep-study review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.

unresolved_source_gaps: People aged 16 years or younger are outside the exact source.

Sleep breathing disorder alias, syncope, acute respiratory distress, nocturnal seizures, generic blood pressure or oropharyngeal examination, apnoea-with-desaturation and respiratory-failure red-flag labels, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some examination fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, severity, driving fitness, device selection, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
